zh.lifeSummary: Johanna Drewe於1556年出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓聖 馬也斯 邱奇。她於1597年與Henry Channon在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓聖 馬也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1610年3月7日享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Drewe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Drew
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a Norman French personal name Dreus based on ancient Germanic Drogo, the name of one of the sons of Charlemagne (compare Gothic driugan ‘to serve as a soldier’). In Old French this name became Dreus, Drues (nominative) and Dru, Driu, Dreu (accusative) and was introduced to England by the Normans. For further information about this name, see also Montague .
English: from the personal name Drew, a pet form of Andrew which was confused with the same name of Old French origin (see 1 above).

Irish: when not an adoption of the English surname, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Druaidh or Ó Druaidh or Ó Draoi ‘son’ and ‘descendant of the druid’, from draoi ‘druid’, genitive druadh or draoi. All of these were often Anglicized as Drury .

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Johanna Drewe was born in 1556, in St Mary's Church, Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Henry Channon in 1597, in St Mary's Church, Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 7 March 1610, at the age of 54.

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna Drewe(은)는 1556년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴 스트 마이'스 c후르치에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1597년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴 스트 마이'스 c후르치에서 Henry Channon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1610년 3월 7일 54세의 나이로 사망했다.
